plugin_desc: HP ProCurve / HP Networking (HPN)-specific SNMP collection for switches (HP-ICF MIBs). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects HP private OIDs for device health: CPU utilization and system memory (used/total). Temperature and fan/power-supply state live in HP's shared ENTITY-SENSOR / hpicfSensorTable that requires per-row type/class filtering, which telegraf inputs.snmp cannot do, so those are not collected (N/A). Memory utilization is computed from used/total. Supported brands/models — HP / HPE ProCurve switches (2500/2600/2900/2920/3500/5400 ProCurve series, etc., enterprise 11).
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the CPU utilization of the HP ProCurve / HP Networking device control plane (HP-ICF hpSwitchCpuStat), normalized to a 0-100 percentage. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate control-plane overload, routing instability, or attack traffic, and may lead to slow management response or packet processing delays.

metrics.name: device_memory_used
metrics.description: This metric reports the number of bytes currently in use by the HP ProCurve / HP Networking system memory (HP-ICF hpLocalMemAllocBytes). It helps administrators understand absolute memory consumption and identify when the device is under memory pressure.

metrics.name: device_memory_total
metrics.description: This metric reports the total size in bytes of the HP ProCurve / HP Networking system memory (HP-ICF hpLocalMemTotalBytes). Together with the used bytes it determines overall memory utilization.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports overall memory utilization of the HP ProCurve / HP Networking device, computed as used / total * 100. High memory usage may indicate memory leaks, oversized routing/MAC/ARP tables, or insufficient capacity, and can cause instability or failed allocations.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.
